task_completion.description: Open the target PCAP file for analysis.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Wireshark main window showing 'sip-rtp-g711.pcap' loaded in the title bar or packet list, or a terminal command actively reading the pcap file.

task_completion.description: Identify and analyze the RTP streams within the capture.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Wireshark 'RTP Streams' window open showing the stream details (e.g., G.711 PCMU payload), or equivalent terminal output analyzing RTP packets.

task_completion.description: Access the audio extraction or playback tool.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Wireshark 'RTP Player' window visible showing the audio stream/waveform, or an equivalent command line tool preparing to extract the payload.

task_completion.description: Save the reconstructed audio to the specified file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save' or 'Export' dialog box showing the filename 'recovered_call.au' and format set to .au / Sun Audio, or a terminal command actively writing to this file.

task_completion.description: Verify the output file exists in the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing 'ls' output or a file manager window displaying 'recovered_call.au' inside the '~/Documents/captures/' directory.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used a genuine network analysis tool (like Wireshark or tshark) to extract the audio.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show actual interaction with Wireshark's GUI or valid tshark/sox commands, not just a text editor or mock script.

integrity.description: Ensure the audio file was extracted from the PCAP, not downloaded from the internet.
integrity.visual_evidence: Absence of web browser downloads, wget, or curl commands fetching the .au file from external URLs.

integrity.description: Verify the file was genuinely created through extraction, not by creating a dummy file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The save action should be linked to the RTP extraction tool. Look for the absence of commands like 'touch recovered_call.au' or simply copying/renaming an unrelated file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The input file is 'sip-rtp-g711.pcap'. The expected output is '/home/ga/Documents/captures/recovered_call.au'. The extracted file must be in Sun/NeXT audio format (.au) and its size should be approximately 144.5 KB (well over the 50KB minimum). The PCAP contains 840 RTP packets encoded in G.711 PCMU.
